seed_instruction: Create a wedding planning timeline for a couple getting married in six months, with monthly tasks.
org_constraint: The response must consist of exactly six paragraphs.
new_constraint: The response must consist of at least eight paragraphs.
instruction: Create a wedding planning timeline for a couple getting married in six months, with monthly tasks. The response must consist of exactly six paragraphs. The response must consist of at least eight paragraphs.
response:
Planning a wedding in six months is an exciting, albeit busy, endeavor, but with a structured timeline, you can navigate the process smoothly. The first month is all about establishing your vision and budget. This is the time to solidify your guest list size—even a preliminary one—and decide on your overall wedding style (formal, casual, rustic, etc.). Research venues immediately, as desirable locations book up fast. Once you have a target date and a rough budget range, start compiling a master vendor list. Begin getting quotes from photographers and videographers, as their schedules fill up quickly.

The second month is dedicated to locking down the biggest elements. Secure your venue and book your top-priority vendors, such as your caterer and photographer/videographer. Once the venue is signed, you can start focusing on the guest experience. Begin researching wedding attire—dress shopping for the bride and suit/tuxedo shopping for the groom. Simultaneously, start exploring different styles for your wedding party and look into potential officiants if needed.

Month three is about filling in the details and booking the remaining major vendors. Secure your DJ or band. Start tasting menus with your caterer and finalize the guest count estimate. Begin planning your ceremony structure and look at different floral design concepts. It is also crucial during this month to research and book transportation (limos, shuttle buses, etc.) if you require it for your guests or wedding party.

The fourth month transitions into the administrative and aesthetic planning phases. Send out your wedding Save-the-Dates to give your guests adequate notice. Start compiling your must-have vendor quotes for secondary elements like makeup artists and rental companies. Finalize your color palette and discuss design details with your florist. If you are having a shower or bachelor/bachelorette party, this is the perfect time to start planning those events.

Month five is intense detail time. Send out formal invitations (or at least the "Save the Dates" if you haven't already, and formal invites if you have a firm headcount). Follow up with all confirmed vendors to review contracts and timelines. Finalize your menu choices, including any dietary restrictions. Begin shopping for wedding accessories, like invitations stationery, rings, and any personalized favors.

The final month before the wedding is about execution and relaxation. Send out a final RSVP deadline reminder. Create a detailed day-of timeline and share it with your wedding party and key vendors. Arrange for any necessary vendor payments or final confirmations. Get your attire professionally cleaned and ensure you have a designated person (a planner or trusted friend) to manage vendor arrivals on the wedding day.<turn|>
